>
> **Why it exists**
>
> Claude Code's HTTP MCP client gives up reconnecting after ~5 attempts. `just refresh` brings panoptd down for longer than that retry budget, so every refresh used to drop the Claude Code session permanently — needing a manual `/mcp` reconnect each time. The proxy decouples the two transports: Claude Code's stdio link stays up as long as the proxy process does (the whole conversation), and panoptd lifecycle becomes invisible.
>
> **How it works (one paragraph)**
>
> - Claude Code spawns `panopt _mcp-proxy --host … --port … --ws … --id … --name … --token …` via `--mcp-config`.
> - Proxy answers `initialize` to Claude Code locally with its own server info, and separately runs `initialize` against panoptd over HTTP to get a panoptd `mcp-session-id`. The URL carries `?agent=…&name=…`, so the daemon's first-sight `enter()` registers the agent with the right display name — no explicit `identify` needed.
> - Every other JSON-RPC frame (`tools/list`, `tools/call`, …) is forwarded verbatim to panoptd. The id is preserved so responses correlate.
> - On any forwarded-call failure (panoptd restarted, session forgotten, connection refused) the proxy drops the panoptd session and re-initializes. Up to 30s of exponential backoff per call. Short outage → slow tool call. Long outage → JSON-RPC error to Claude Code.
> - On stdin EOF (Claude Code closed the session) the proxy calls `agent_leave` to clear its registry entry and locks, then exits.
>
> **How to use it**
>
> You don't, directly — it's a `_`-prefixed hidden subcommand. Everything that needs the proxy gets it transparently:
>
> - **Cockpit agents** (`panopt agent foo`): the env-templated `mcp.rs` config sends Claude Code through the proxy automatically.
> - **Hand-launched agents** (`claude --mcp-config "$(panopt agent-config --name greg-main)"`): `agent-config` emits the same stdio config; the proxy starts when claude does.
> - **The current session you're in right now**: if it was started before the proxy landed, it's still on the old http transport. New panes pick up the proxy the next time `panopt _agent` writes `mcp.rs` via `mcp::ensure()`.
>
> **Operational notes**
>
> - **Diagnose a stuck call**: the proxy writes one-line errors to stderr. Stdin/stdout carry the JSON-RPC traffic. Claude Code captures the stderr — if a call is hanging, check Claude Code's MCP debug log for proxy chatter.
> - **The viewer panes still talk to panoptd over HTTP directly**, not through this proxy. Their churn (todo #85) is unrelated to the proxy's behavior — Claude Code is the only piece that needed shielding.
> - **`agent_leave` on exit isn't bulletproof**: if the proxy is SIGKILL'd, the registry entry lingers until idle-prune (session keys) or daemon restart (declared keys). Normal close paths — Claude Code quitting cleanly, stdin EOF — fire it.
> - **No state**: the proxy keeps no on-disk state. Every restart starts fresh.

addedBlocks:
## panopt
PANopt coordination daemon: shared todos and scratchpads across agents.
Each connection is scoped to one project by the ?ws=<project path> query parameter on the server URL.
- Registry: call identify (name, optional status) on startup so others can see you; whoami returns your own entry; agent_list returns every agent on the project (each entry's key_source is `declared` for stable `?agent=<id>` ids or `session` for the rotating MCP session id - only session entries idle-prune). Connecting already registers you. agent_leave removes your registry entry and releases your locks; the idle sweep handles agents that just disappear.
- Locks: lock_acquire (name, optional note) takes a named advisory lock and is non-blocking; lock_release frees it; lock_status lists every held lock. Locks are advisory - agents cooperate, the daemon does not enforce.
- Todos: todo_create (returns an id), todo_list (summaries), todo_get (one todo in full), todo_update (edit any field), todo_complete, todo_delete, todo_add_blocker / todo_remove_blocker / todo_set_blockers, todo_comment_add / todo_comment_update / todo_comment_delete, todo_tags_list (project tag vocabulary, union with scratchpads), and todo_lock / todo_unlock (advisory `todo:<id>` lock, surfaced as locked_by on todo_get). Each todo also projects to .panopt/todos/<id>.md.
- Scratchpads: scratchpad_create (returns an id), scratchpad_list, scratchpad_get (one scratchpad in full), scratchpad_append (add to body), scratchpad_read (body only), scratchpad_update (replace title, body, and/or tags), scratchpad_delete, scratchpad_tags_list (project tag vocabulary, same union as todo_tags_list). Each scratchpad also projects to .panopt/scratchpad/<id>.md.
- Agent tools (config layer): agent_tool_create (returns an id), agent_tool_list, agent_tool_get, agent_tool_update, agent_tool_delete. Durable per-project agent configurations the cockpit can spawn from. Projected to .panopt/agent_tools.md.
- Processes (instance layer): process_create (kind agent/command/terminal, optional agent_tool_… [truncated]

input.title: panopt MCP proxy — what it is and how to use it

input.body:
# panopt MCP proxy

## What it is

`panopt _mcp-proxy` is a tiny long-lived process Claude Code spawns at session start. It speaks **MCP-over-stdio** to Claude Code and **MCP-over-HTTP** to panoptd, sitting between them as a forwarder. From Claude Code's point of view, the proxy *is* panoptd — and the proxy hides every panoptd restart, session-id rotation, and brief outage from Claude Code's connection layer.

## Why it exists

Claude Code's HTTP MCP client gives up reconnecting after ~5 attempts. `just refresh` brings panoptd down for longer than that retry budget, so every refresh used to drop the Claude Code session permanently — needing a manual `/mcp` reconnect each time.

The proxy decouples the two transports: Claude Code's stdio link stays up as long as the proxy process does (the whole conversation), and panoptd lifecycle becomes invisible.

## How it works

- Claude Code spawns `panopt _mcp-proxy --host … --port … --ws … --id … --name … --token …` via the `--mcp-config` it reads at startup.
- Proxy answers `initialize` to Claude Code locally with its own server info (`name: "panopt-proxy"`), and *separately* runs `initialize` against panoptd over HTTP to get a panoptd `mcp-session-id`. The URL carries `?agent=…&name=…`, so the daemon's first-sight `enter()` registers the agent with the right display name — no explicit `identify` call needed from the proxy.
- Every other JSON-RPC frame (`tools/list`, `tools/call`, future methods) is forwarded verbatim to panoptd. The JSON-RPC `id` is preserved so responses correlate on Claude Code's side without rewrite.
- On any forwarded-call failure (panoptd restarted, session forgotten, connection refused) the proxy drops the panoptd session and re-initializes. Up to 30s of exponential backoff per call. Short outage → slow tool call, no error. Longer outage → JSON-RPC error to Claude Code, conversation continues with one failed call.
- On stdin EOF (Claude Code closed the session) the proxy calls `agent_leave` to clear its registry entry and locks, then exits.

## How to use it

You don't, directly — it's a `_`-prefixed hidden subcommand. Everything that needs the proxy gets it transparently:

- **Cockpit agents** (`panopt agent foo`): the env-templated `mcp.rs` config sends Claude Code through the proxy automatically.
- **Hand-launched agents** (`claude --mcp-config "$(panopt agent-config --name greg-main)"`): `agent-config` emits the same stdio config; the proxy starts when claude does.
- **A Claude Code session that was already running when the proxy landed**: it's still on the old HTTP transport. New agent panes pick up the proxy the next time `panopt _agent` writes `mcp.rs` via `mcp::ensure()`. To opt the current session in, restart the pane.

## Operational notes

- **Diagnose a stuck call**: the proxy writes one-line errors to stderr. Stdin/stdout carry the JSON-RPC traffic. Claude Code captures stderr — if a call is hanging, check Claude Code's MCP debug log for proxy chatter.
- **Viewer panes still talk to panoptd over HTTP directly**, not through this proxy. Their connect/close churn (todo #85) is unrelated to the proxy's behavior — Claude Code is the only piece that needed shielding from daemon lifecycle.
- **`agent_leave` on exit isn't bulletproof**: if the proxy is SIGKILL'd (process supervisor kills it, OOM, etc.), the registry entry lingers until idle-prune (for `session` keys) or daemon restart (for `declared` keys). Normal close paths — Claude Code quitting cleanly, stdin EOF, Ctrl-C of the agent pane — all fire `agent_leave`.
- **No state on disk**: the proxy keeps no state. Every restart starts fresh; the only durable thing is the `?agent=…` id baked into the URL.
- **One proxy per Claude Code session**: don't share a proxy across sessions. Each session gets its own stdio child.

## Code pointers

- `crates/panopt/src/mcp_proxy.rs` — the proxy itself (`run`, `Backend`, `dispatch`).
- `crates/panopt/src/mcp.rs` — the stdio config template embedded in `panopt _agent`.
- `crates/panopt/src/agent_config.rs` — the equivalent for hand-launched sessions.
- `crates/panopt/src/main.rs` — `Cmd::McpProxy` wiring (search for `_mcp-proxy`).

 - `panopt-core` — transport-agnostic state, persistence (SQLite), filesystem projection. **Must not depend on `rmcp`, `axum`, or `tokio`** — this boundary is the reason core is reusable. Verify with `cargo tree -p panopt-core` if you add a dep.
-- `panoptd` — MCP daemon over Streamable HTTP on `127.0.0.1:7600`. Wraps `panopt-core` in a `Mutex`.
+- `panoptd` — MCP daemon over Streamable HTTP. Defaults to `127.0.0.1:7600`; bind is configurable via `--host` and every request is gated by a bearer token at `~/.local/share/panopt/token` (`Authorization: Bearer <token>` or `?token=<token>`). Wraps `panopt-core` in a `Mutex`.
 - `panopt` — CLI launcher and viewer. Starts `panoptd` on demand, drives Zellij, spawns agent panes.
 - `panopt-zellij` — Zellij sidebar plugin. **Excluded from the cargo workspace** because it only builds for `wasm32-wasip1`.

 The daemon exposes todos, scratchpads, locks, agent registry, and roster as MCP tools. Each MCP connection is scoped to one project via `?ws=<absolute-path>` on the URL. State mirrors to `.panopt/*.md` on every mutation — those files are read-mostly for humans and the Zellij plugin, not a write surface.
 
+## Hand-launching a first-class agent
+
+Cockpit-spawned panes are first-class automatically (the launcher injects `PANOPT_AGENT`, `PANOPT_NAME`, `PANOPT_TOKEN` into the pane env). For a Claude Code session started outside the cockpit — or on another machine — use `panopt agent-config` so the session lands with a stable id, a friendly display name, and the bearer token in one shot:
+
+```
+claude --mcp-config "$(panopt agent-config --name greg-main)"
+```
+
+Without this, the session falls back to the rotating `mcp-session-id` HTTP header as its agent key. The key churns on every reconnect, locks held under old keys get reaped by the 30s idle sweep, and other agents see the connection as a series of unreferenceable UUIDs. See `crates/panopt/src/agent_config.rs` for the resolver defaults (id = `$USER-$HOSTNAME`).
+
 ## Working a panopt todo
 
 When the user asks you to work on a todo (`do #N`, `start #N`, `work on #N`, etc.), call `mcp__panopt__todo_start` as your first MCP action against that todo *instead of* `todo_get`. It returns the same full detail, flips status to `in_progress`, and claims the `todo:<N>` advisory lock so other agents and the sidebar see the project state correctly. When you finish, call `todo_complete`. If `todo_start` returns `{started: false, held_by: <name>}`, stop and tell the user — another agent already owns it.

1	//! The MCP server handler: the coordination tools and the `ServerHandler`
2	//! impl that advertises them.
3	//!
4	//! Every tool resolves its project from the `ws` query parameter on the
5	//! request URL (DESIGN.md Section 5.3) and registers the calling agent by its
6	//! agent key - the `agent` URL parameter when set, the MCP session id
7	//! otherwise - so one daemon serves every project at once with no per-session
8	//! state welded into the handler.
9	
10	use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
11	
12	use http::request::Parts;
13	use panopt_core::{
14	    Agent, AgentTool, AgentToolPatch, CoreError, Lock, Priority, Process, ProcessKind,
15	    ProcessPatch, ProjectId, Scratchpad, ScratchpadPatch, Store, Todo, TodoPatch, TodoStatus,
16	};
17	use rmcp::{
18	    handler::server::{common::Extension, router::tool::ToolRouter, wrapper::Parameters},
19	    model::*,
20	    tool, tool_handler, tool_router,
21	    ErrorData as McpError, ServerHandler,
22	};
23	use serde::Serialize;
24	
25	use crate::params::{
26	    AgentToolCreateArgs, AgentToolDeleteArgs, AgentToolGetArgs, AgentToolUpdateArgs, IdKindArgs,
27	    IdentifyArgs, LockAcquireArgs, LockReleaseArgs, ProcessCreateArgs, ProcessDeleteArgs,
28	    ProcessGetArgs, ProcessUpdateArgs, ScratchpadAppendArgs, ScratchpadCreateArgs,
29	    ScratchpadDeleteArgs, ScratchpadGetArgs, ScratchpadReadArgs, ScratchpadUpdateArgs,
30	    TodoBlockerArgs, TodoCommentAddArgs, TodoCommentDeleteArgs, TodoCommentUpdateArgs,
31	    TodoCompleteArgs, TodoCreateArgs, TodoDeleteArgs, TodoGetArgs, TodoLockArgs,
32	    TodoSetBlockersArgs, TodoStartArgs, TodoUnlockArgs, TodoUpdateArgs,
33	};
34	
35	/// Per-session MCP handler.
36	///
37	/// rmcp builds one of these per MCP session via the factory in `main`. Each
38	/// holds a *clone of the shared `Arc`*, so every session - and therefore every
39	/// connected agent - mutates and reads the one `Mutex<Store>`. The session
40	/// carries no project: each tool call derives it from the request URL.
41	#[derive(Clone)]
42	pub struct Handler {
43	    state: Arc<Mutex<Store>>,
44	    // Part of rmcp's `#[tool_router]` / `#[tool_handler]` pattern: built once in
45	    // `new()` and consulted by the macro-generated `ServerHandler` impl. The
46	    // dead-code lint does not attribute that macro-generated use to the field.
47	    #[allow(dead_code)]
48	    tool_router: ToolRouter<Self>,
49	}
50	
51	/// Wire shape for a scratchpad in `scratchpad_list` output.
52	#[derive(Serialize)]
53	struct ScratchpadDto {
54	    id: u64,
55	    title: String,
56	}
57	
58	/// Wire shape for `scratchpad_get`: title, body, tags, and timestamps.
59	#[derive(Serialize)]
60	struct ScratchpadDetailDto {
61	    id: u64,
62	    title: String,
63	    body: String,
64	    tags: Vec<String>,
65	    created_at: String,
66	    updated_at: String,
67	}
68	
69	impl ScratchpadDetailDto {
70	    fn from_scratchpad(pad: &Scratchpad) -> Self {
71	        ScratchpadDetailDto {
72	            id: pad.id,
73	            title: pad.title.clone(),
74	            body: pad.body.clone(),
75	            tags: pad.tags.clone(),
76	            created_at: pad.created_at.clone(),
77	            updated_at: pad.updated_at.clone(),
78	        }
79	    }
80	}
81	
82	/// Wire shape for `id_kind`: the resource kind and a short human label
83	/// (title for todos/scratchpads, `display_name` falling back to `name` for
84	/// agent tools and processes - the same fallback the cockpit's projection
85	/// uses).
86	#[derive(Serialize)]
87	struct IdKindDto {
88	    kind: &'static str,
89	    label: String,
90	}
91	
92	/// Wire shape for a todo in `todo_list` output: every field but the body and
93	/// the full comment thread, which `todo_get` returns.
94	#[derive(Serialize)]
95	struct TodoSummaryDto {
96	    id: u64,
97	    title: String,
98	    status: &'static str,
99	    priority: &'static str,
100	    assignee: String,
101	    tags: Vec<String>,
102	    blockers: Vec<u64>,
103	    comment_count: usize,
104	    created_at: String,
105	    updated_at: String,
106	}
107	
108	impl TodoSummaryDto {
109	    fn from_todo(todo: &Todo) -> Self {
110	        TodoSummaryDto {
111	            id: todo.id,
112	            title: todo.title.clone(),
113	            status: todo.status.as_str(),
114	            priority: todo.priority.as_str(),
115	            assignee: todo.assignee.clone(),
116	            tags: todo.tags.clone(),
117	            blockers: todo.blockers.clone(),

119	            created_at: todo.created_at.clone(),
120	            updated_at: todo.updated_at.clone(),
121	        }
122	    }
123	}
124	
125	/// Wire shape for one comment in `todo_get` output.
126	#[derive(Serialize)]
127	struct TodoCommentDto {
128	    id: u64,
129	    author: String,
130	    body: String,
131	    created_at: String,
132	}
133	
134	/// Wire shape for the full todo returned by `todo_get`.
135	#[derive(Serialize)]
136	struct TodoDetailDto {
137	    id: u64,
138	    title: String,
139	    body: String,
140	    status: &'static str,
141	    priority: &'static str,
142	    assignee: String,
143	    tags: Vec<String>,
144	    blockers: Vec<u64>,
145	    comments: Vec<TodoCommentDto>,
146	    created_at: String,
147	    updated_at: String,
148	    completed_at: Option<String>,
149	    /// Display name of the agent currently holding `todo:<id>` as an advisory
150	    /// lock; `None` when no agent holds it. Locks are ephemeral and not part of
151	    /// the SQLite-stored todo, so the daemon resolves this at read time.
152	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
153	    locked_by: Option<String>,
154	}
155	
156	impl TodoDetailDto {
157	    fn from_todo(todo: Todo, locked_by: Option<String>) -> Self {
158	        TodoDetailDto {
159	            id: todo.id,
160	            title: todo.title,
161	            body: todo.body,
162	            status: todo.status.as_str(),
163	            priority: todo.priority.as_str(),
164	            assignee: todo.assignee,

176	                .collect(),

179	            completed_at: todo.completed_at,

181	        }
182	    }
183	}

1	//! Plain data types for PANopt's shared state.
2	//!
3	//! These carry no serialization or transport concerns; `panoptd` maps them onto
4	//! its own wire shapes at the protocol boundary.
5	
6	use std::time::SystemTime;
7	
8	/// Opaque handle to a project in the [`crate::Store`].
9	///
10	/// Minted by [`crate::Store::ensure_project`] and passed back into store
11	/// methods to scope them to one project. Callers treat it as a token - they
12	/// never construct or inspect it - so the inner row id stays private.
13	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
14	pub struct ProjectId(pub(crate) i64);
15	
16	/// A shared, append-oriented note identified by a stable numeric id.
17	///
18	/// The `id` (not the `title`) is the durable handle and the projected filename,
19	/// so renaming a scratchpad never moves its file. The id is unique within its
20	/// project and restarts at 1 in each project.
21	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
22	pub struct Scratchpad {
23	    pub id: u64,
24	    pub title: String,
25	    pub body: String,
26	    /// Free-text labels drawn from the project's tag vocabulary, shared with
27	    /// todos (todo #61): [`crate::Store::tags_list`] returns the union across
28	    /// both kinds, so a tag set on one surface is offered up on the other.
29	    pub tags: Vec<String>,
30	    pub created_at: String,
31	    pub updated_at: String,
32	}
33	
34	/// A set of optional edits to a [`Scratchpad`], applied by
35	/// [`crate::Store::scratchpad_update`].
36	///
37	/// Each `None` field leaves that attribute untouched; each `Some` replaces it.
38	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
39	pub struct ScratchpadPatch {
40	    pub title: Option<String>,
41	    pub body: Option<String>,
42	    pub tags: Option<Vec<String>>,
43	}
44	
45	/// Lifecycle state of a [`Todo`].
46	///
47	/// The variants mirror Solo's `todos.status` column (DESIGN.md Section
48	/// 6.1) plus two panopt-specific additions, `Draft` and `NotDone`:
49	/// `Draft` is an early note the author has not yet committed to doing,
50	/// `Backlog` is not yet scheduled, `Open` is ready to pick up,
51	/// `InProgress` is being worked, `Completed` is done, and `NotDone`
52	/// records that the todo was closed without being done (cancelled,
53	/// won't-fix, superseded). Both `Completed` and `NotDone` are terminal;
54	/// only `Completed` sets `completed_at`.
55	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
56	pub enum TodoStatus {
57	    #[default]
58	    Open,
59	    InProgress,
60	    Backlog,
61	    Draft,
62	    Completed,
63	    NotDone,
64	}
65	
66	impl TodoStatus {
67	    /// The token stored in SQLite and used on the wire and in the projection.
68	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
69	        match self {
70	            TodoStatus::Open => "open",
71	            TodoStatus::InProgress => "in_progress",
72	            TodoStatus::Backlog => "backlog",
73	            TodoStatus::Draft => "draft",
74	            TodoStatus::Completed => "completed",
75	            TodoStatus::NotDone => "not_done",
76	        }
77	    }
78	
79	    /// Parse a stored or wire token; `None` for an unrecognized string.
80	    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<TodoStatus> {
81	        match s {
82	            "open" => Some(TodoStatus::Open),
83	            "in_progress" => Some(TodoStatus::InProgress),
84	            "backlog" => Some(TodoStatus::Backlog),
85	            "draft" => Some(TodoStatus::Draft),
86	            "completed" => Some(TodoStatus::Completed),
87	            "not_done" => Some(TodoStatus::NotDone),
88	            _ => None,
89	        }
90	    }
91	}
92	
93	/// Importance of a [`Todo`], mirroring Solo's `todos.priority` column.
94	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
95	pub enum Priority {
96	    High,
97	    #[default]
98	    Medium,
99	    Low,
100	}
101	
102	impl Priority {
103	    /// The token stored in SQLite and used on the wire and in the projection.
104	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
105	        match self {
106	            Priority::High => "high",
107	            Priority::Medium => "medium",
108	            Priority::Low => "low",
109	        }
110	    }
111	
112	    /// Parse a stored or wire token; `None` for an unrecognized string.
113	    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<Priority> {
114	        match s {
115	            "high" => Some(Priority::High),
116	            "medium" => Some(Priority::Medium),
117	            "low" => Some(Priority::Low),
118	            _ => None,
119	        }
120	    }
121	}
122	
123	/// One comment on a [`Todo`], mirroring a row of Solo's `todo_comments` table.
124	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
125	pub struct TodoComment {
126	    /// Id unique within the parent todo, restarting at 1 in each todo.
127	    pub id: u64,
128	    /// Display name of the agent that posted it.
129	    pub author: String,
130	    pub body: String,
131	    /// SQLite `datetime('now')` text (UTC, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`).
132	    pub created_at: String,
133	}
134	
135	/// A todo item identified by a numeric id, unique within its project.
136	///
137	/// The field set mirrors a trimmed subset of Solo's `todos` table plus its
138	/// `todo_comments` and `todo_blockers` side tables (DESIGN.md Section 6.1).
139	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
140	pub struct Todo {
141	    pub id: u64,
142	    pub title: String,
143	    /// Free-form description. Empty until set.
144	    pub body: String,
145	    pub status: TodoStatus,
146	    pub priority: Priority,
147	    /// Free-text owner; empty when unassigned. Unlike Solo's agent foreign key
148	    /// this is a plain name: PANopt's agent registry is in-memory and ephemeral
149	    /// (DESIGN.md Section 6.3), so a persisted agent id would dangle.
150	    pub assignee: String,
151	    pub tags: Vec<String>,
152	    /// Ids of other todos in the same project that block this one.
153	    pub blockers: Vec<u64>,
154	    pub comments: Vec<TodoComment>,
155	    /// SQLite `datetime('now')` text (UTC).
156	    pub created_at: String,
157	    pub updated_at: String,
158	    /// Set while `status` is `Completed`, `None` otherwise.
159	    pub completed_at: Option<String>,
160	}
161	
162	/// A set of optional edits to a [`Todo`], applied by [`crate::Store::todo_update`].
163	///
164	/// Each `None` field leaves that attribute untouched; each `Some` replaces it.
165	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
166	pub struct TodoPatch {
167	    pub title: Option<String>,
168	    pub body: Option<String>,
169	    pub status: Option<TodoStatus>,
170	    pub priority: Option<Priority>,
171	    pub assignee: Option<String>,
172	    pub tags: Option<Vec<String>>,
173	}
174	
175	/// What a [`Process`] is - an agent CLI, a project command, or a plain
176	/// terminal. Modeled on the values of Solo's `processes.kind` column.
177	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
178	pub enum ProcessKind {
179	    #[default]
180	    Agent,
181	    Command,
182	    Terminal,
183	}
184	
185	impl ProcessKind {
186	    /// The token stored in SQLite and used on the wire and in the projection.
187	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
188	        match self {
189	            ProcessKind::Agent => "agent",
190	            ProcessKind::Command => "command",
191	            ProcessKind::Terminal => "terminal",
192	        }
193	    }
194	
195	    /// Parse a stored or wire token; `None` for an unrecognized string.
196	    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<ProcessKind> {
197	        match s {
198	            "agent" => Some(ProcessKind::Agent),
199	            "command" => Some(ProcessKind::Command),
200	            "terminal" => Some(ProcessKind::Terminal),
201	            _ => None,
202	        }
203	    }
204	}
205	
206	/// A durable, per-project agent configuration: the "what to launch" half of
207	/// the two-layer process model (todo #27). One tool can back many running
208	/// [`Process`] instances. Modeled on a row of Solo's `agent_tools` table.
209	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
210	pub struct AgentTool {
211	    /// Numeric id, unique within the project, drawn from the unified
212	    /// `projects.next_id` counter (todo #16).

lines: /// `panopt agent [name]` - open a new agent pane in the running cockpit.
/// `panopt _agent` - the entrypoint that runs inside an agent pane.
/// Sets the per-agent environment and replaces this process with `claude`, so
-/// the pane *is* the agent with no PANopt wrapper around it.
+/// the pane *is* the agent with no PANopt wrapper around it. The MCP template
+/// at `mcp.rs` expands these env vars in the `--mcp-config` URL, giving the
+/// pane a stable agent id, a friendly display name, and the bearer token.

1	//! `panopt` - the PANopt cockpit launcher.
2	//!
3	//! Brings up the coordination daemon on demand and the Zellij cockpit, and
4	//! opens agent panes - each with a stable per-agent identity (DESIGN.md §9).
5	
6	mod agent;
7	mod agent_tool;
8	mod close_gate;
9	mod daemon;
10	mod delete_gate;
11	mod edit;
12	mod id_kind;
13	mod mcp;
14	mod mcpclient;
15	mod paths;
16	mod process;
17	mod scratchpad;
18	mod scratchpad_form;
19	mod todo;
20	mod todo_form;
21	mod up;
22	mod viewer;
23	mod viewstate;
24	mod wrap;
25	
26	use std::path::PathBuf;
27	
28	use clap::{Parser, Subcommand};
29	
30	#[derive(Parser)]
31	#[command(name = "panopt", about = "PANopt cockpit launcher", version)]
32	struct Cli {
33	    /// Port the panopt daemon listens on.
34	    #[arg(long, default_value_t = 7600, global = true)]
35	    port: u16,
36	
37	    #[command(subcommand)]
38	    command: Cmd,
39	}
40	
41	#[derive(Subcommand)]
42	enum Cmd {
43	    /// Boot the cockpit: the daemon, a Zellij session, the sidebar, and an
44	    /// agent. Attaches if the cockpit is already running.
45	    Up {
46	        /// Path to the panopt-zellij plugin `.wasm` (auto-detected from a dev
47	        /// build when omitted).
48	        #[arg(long)]
49	        plugin: Option<PathBuf>,
50	    },
51	    /// Open a new agent pane in the running cockpit.
52	    Agent {
53	        /// Optional readable name for the agent.
54	        name: Option<String>,
55	    },
56	    /// Inspect and edit the project's shared todos.
57	    Todo {
58	        /// Project root the todos belong to (default: the current directory).
59	        #[arg(long, global = true)]
60	        ws: Option<PathBuf>,
61	        #[command(subcommand)]
62	        action: todo::TodoCmd,
63	    },
64	    /// Inspect and edit the project's agent tools (durable spawn configs).
65	    #[command(name = "agent-tool")]
66	    AgentTool {
67	        /// Project root the agent tools belong to (default: the current directory).
68	        #[arg(long, global = true)]
69	        ws: Option<PathBuf>,
70	        #[command(subcommand)]
71	        action: agent_tool::AgentToolCmd,
72	    },
73	    /// Inspect and edit the project's processes (agent/command/terminal instances).
74	    Process {
75	        /// Project root the processes belong to (default: the current directory).
76	        #[arg(long, global = true)]
77	        ws: Option<PathBuf>,
78	        #[command(subcommand)]
79	        action: process::ProcessCmd,
80	    },
81	    /// Operate on the project's scratchpads from the CLI. Currently exposes
82	    /// only `rm`; the rest of the surface lives in the cockpit's editor form.
83	    Scratchpad {
84	        /// Project root the scratchpads belong to (default: the current directory).
85	        #[arg(long, global = true)]
86	        ws: Option<PathBuf>,
87	        #[command(subcommand)]
88	        action: scratchpad::ScratchpadCmd,
89	    },
90	    /// Resolve a numeric id to its resource kind (todo / scratchpad /
91	    /// agent-tool / process) via the daemon's `id_kind` MCP tool.
92	    #[command(name = "id-kind")]
93	    IdKind {
94	        /// Project root the id belongs to (default: the current directory).
95	        #[arg(long)]
96	        ws: Option<PathBuf>,
97	        /// Numeric id to resolve.
98	        id: u64,
99	    },
100	    /// Internal: the entrypoint that runs inside an agent pane.
101	    #[command(name = "_agent", hide = true)]
102	    AgentExec {
103	        #[arg(long)]
104	        ws: Option<PathBuf>,
105	        #[arg(long)]
106	        id: Option<String>,
107	    },
108	    /// Internal: start a process in this pane.
109	    #[command(name = "_process-run", hide = true)]
110	    ProcessRun {
111	        #[arg(long)]
112	        ws: Option<PathBuf>,
113	        /// Numeric id of the process to start.
114	        id: u64,
115	    },
116	    /// Internal: a long-lived cockpit viewer pane.
117	    #[command(name = "_viewer", hide = true)]
118	    ViewerExec {
119	        #[arg(long)]
120	        ws: Option<PathBuf>,
121	        /// Routing-file token the sidebar plugin assigned this pane.
122	        #[arg(long)]
123	        slot: String,
124	        /// Initial item kind: todo, scratchpad, todo-list, scratchpad-list.
125	        #[arg(long)]
126	        kind: Option<String>,
127	        /// Initial item id, for the todo and scratchpad kinds.
128	        #[arg(long)]
129	        id: Option<u64>,
130	    },
131	    /// Internal: the floating close-gate dialog the sidebar plugin spawns
132	    /// when a destructive action would lose active items.
133	    #[command(name = "_close-gate", hide = true)]
134	    CloseGateExec {
135	        /// What the user tried to do: focus, tab, or quit.
136	        #[arg(long)]
137	        scope: String,
138	        /// Active items the dialog shows: `kind:label;kind:label;...`.
139	        #[arg(long, default_value = "")]
140	        items: String,
141	        /// Terminal pane id to close when scope is `focus`.
142	        #[arg(long)]
143	        target_pane: Option<u32>,
144	    },
145	    /// Internal: the floating delete-confirmation dialog the sidebar plugin
146	    /// spawns when the user presses `x` on a row.
147	    #[command(name = "_delete-gate", hide = true)]
148	    DeleteGateExec {
149	        /// Item kind: todo, scratchpad, agent-tool, or process.
150	        #[arg(long)]
151	        kind: String,
152	        /// Numeric id of the item the user wants to delete.
153	        #[arg(long)]
154	        id: u64,
155	        /// Human label for the row (title / name); shown in the dialog.
156	        #[arg(long, default_value = "")]
157	        label: String,
158	    },
159	}
160	
161	fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
162	    let cli = Cli::parse();
163	    match cli.command {
164	        Cmd::Up { plugin } => up::run(plugin, cli.port),
165	        Cmd::Agent { name } => agent::spawn(name),
166	        Cmd::Todo { ws, action } => todo::run(ws, action, cli.port),
167	        Cmd::AgentTool { ws, action } => agent_tool::run(ws, action, cli.port),
168	        Cmd::Process { ws, action } => process::run(ws, action, cli.port),
169	        Cmd::Scratchpad { ws, action } => scratchpad::run(ws, action, cli.port),
170	        Cmd::IdKind { ws, id } => id_kind::run(ws, id, cli.port),
171	        Cmd::AgentExec { ws, id } => agent::exec_in_pane(ws, id, cli.port),
172	        Cmd::ProcessRun { ws, id } => process::exec_entry(ws, id, cli.port),
173	        Cmd::ViewerExec { ws, slot, kind, id } => viewer::run(ws, cli.port, slot, kind, id),
174	        Cmd::CloseGateExec {
175	            scope,
176	            items,
177	            target_pane,
178	        } => close_gate::run(&scope, &items, target_pane, cli.port),
179	        Cmd::DeleteGateExec { kind, id, label } => delete_gate::run(&kind, id, &label, cli.port),
180	    }
181	}

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Bearer-token auth, --host, and `panopt agent-config` for multi-machine agents

The daemon now gates every request on a bearer token at
`~/.local/share/panopt/token` (Authorization header preferred, ?token=
query param as a fallback), and `--host` lets it bind to addresses other
than loopback - so panoptd can serve agents on other machines on the
same trusted network. Cockpit-spawned agents get the token from env
(PANOPT_TOKEN) the launcher injects; hand-launched ones (`claude
--mcp-config "$(panopt agent-config --name greg-main)"`) get an
equivalent URL with the token baked in, including a stable agent id and
friendly display name, so they look identical to cockpit-spawned panes
in the registry. AGENT_MAX_IDLE bumped to 30 min so a quiet conversation
no longer drops an agent's identity mid-flight.
EOF
)"
